questions.question: Who was strengthened in his kingdom at the beginning of 2 Chronicles?
questions.explanation: 2 Chronicles 1:1 says Solomon the son of David was strengthened in his kingdom.

questions.question: At which high place did Solomon go because the tabernacle was there?
questions.explanation: Solomon went to the high place at Gibeon, where the tabernacle was, in 2 Chronicles 1.

questions.question: Who had brought the ark of God up from Kirjath-jearim?
questions.explanation: David brought the ark up from Kirjath-jearim, as stated in 2 Chronicles 1:4.

questions.question: How many burnt offerings did Solomon offer on the bronze altar at Gibeon?
questions.explanation: Solomon offered a thousand burnt offerings before the Lord at Gibeon in 2 Chronicles 1.

questions.question: Who appeared to Solomon in the night and told him to ask what God should give him?
questions.explanation: God appeared to Solomon at night and invited him to ask for what he wanted in 2 Chronicles 1.

questions.question: What did Solomon request from God?
questions.explanation: Solomon asked for wisdom and knowledge to judge God's people in 2 Chronicles 1.

questions.question: What three things did Solomon NOT ask for before requesting wisdom and knowledge?
questions.explanation: Solomon did not ask for riches, wealth, or honour, but for wisdom and knowledge instead.

questions.question: What did God say He would give Solomon because he asked for wisdom?
questions.correct_answer: Wisdom and knowledge, riches, wealth, and honour
options: Wisdom and knowledge, riches, wealth, and honour
questions.explanation: Because Solomon asked for wisdom and not riches or honor, God promised him wisdom plus riches, wealth, and honor.

questions.question: How many chariots did Solomon have?
questions.explanation: Solomon had 1,400 chariots according to 2 Chronicles 1:14.

questions.question: How many horsemen did Solomon place in chariot cities and Jerusalem?
questions.explanation: Solomon had twelve thousand horsemen placed in the chariot cities and with the king in Jerusalem.

questions.question: What did Solomon make as plentiful as stones in Jerusalem?
questions.explanation: Solomon made silver and gold plenteous as stones in Jerusalem.

questions.question: From which country did Solomon bring horses?
questions.explanation: Solomon brought horses from Egypt in 2 Chronicles 1.

questions.question: What two kinds of houses did Solomon determine to build?
questions.correct_answer: A house for the name of the LORD and a house for his kingdom
options: A house for the name of the LORD and a house for his kingdom
A house for the priests and a house for the Levites
A house for the ark and a house for the people
A house for wealth and a house for war
questions.explanation: Solomon decided to build a house for the Lord and a house for his kingdom.

questions.question: How many men did Solomon tell out to bear burdens?
questions.explanation: Solomon appointed seventy thousand men to bear burdens in the temple work.

questions.question: How many men did Solomon set to hew in the mountain?
questions.explanation: Eighty thousand were appointed to hew in the mountain for the temple construction.

questions.question: How many overseers did Solomon appoint over the laborers?
questions.explanation: Three thousand six hundred were set over Solomon's laborers.

questions.question: Whom did Solomon send to for help in building the house?
questions.explanation: Solomon sent to Huram king of Tyre for help in building the temple.

questions.question: What was one of the purposes of the house Solomon built?
questions.correct_answer: To burn sweet incense before God
options: To burn sweet incense before God
questions.explanation: The temple was built to burn sweet incense before the Lord.

questions.question: How does Solomon describe God in relation to other gods?
questions.correct_answer: Our God is greater than all gods
options: Our God is greater than all gods
Our God is one of many gods
Our God is a warrior god
questions.explanation: Solomon recognizes God as great above all gods in 2 Chronicles 2.

questions.question: Why does Solomon say he is building the house?
questions.explanation: Solomon built the temple to burn sacrifices and worship God.

questions.question: What qualities did Solomon request in the man sent for temple work?
questions.correct_answer: Skilled in gold, silver, brass, iron, purple, crimson, and blue
options: Skilled in gold, silver, brass, iron, purple, crimson, and blue
questions.explanation: Solomon asked for craftsmen skilled with metals, cloth, and engraving.

questions.question: From where did Solomon ask for cedar, fir, and algum trees?
questions.explanation: Solomon requested timber from Lebanon for the temple.

questions.question: How many measures of beaten wheat did Solomon offer to the workers from Tyre?
questions.explanation: Solomon supplied twenty thousand measures of beaten wheat to those who worked on the timber.

questions.question: What did Huram call Solomon in his letter?
questions.correct_answer: A wise son endued with prudence and understanding
options: A wise son endued with prudence and understanding
questions.explanation: Huram praised Solomon as wise and understanding in his letter.

questions.question: What kind of man did Huram send to Solomon?
questions.correct_answer: A cunning man endowed with understanding
options: A cunning man endowed with understanding
questions.explanation: Huram sent a skilled craftsman with wisdom to help build the temple.

questions.question: Of which people was the mother of Huram's craftsman?
questions.explanation: The craftsman's mother was from the daughters of Dan.

questions.question: How many strangers did Solomon number in the land of Israel?
questions.explanation: Solomon counted one hundred fifty-three thousand six hundred strangers in Israel.

questions.question: On what mount did Solomon begin to build the house of the LORD?
questions.explanation: Solomon built the temple on Mount Moriah, where the Lord had appeared to David.

questions.question: In which year of Solomon's reign did he begin to build the temple?
questions.explanation: Solomon started building the temple in the fourth year of his reign.

questions.question: How long was the porch at the front of the temple?
questions.explanation: The porch was twenty cubits long according to 2 Chronicles 3.

questions.question: What material did Solomon overlay the greater house with?
questions.explanation: The greater house was overlaid with pure gold.

questions.question: How long were the wings of the cherubims in the most holy place?
questions.explanation: Each cherubim had wings that spread twenty cubits.

questions.question: How many cubits long were the wings of each cherub?
questions.explanation: Each wing was five cubits long and reached to the wall or the other cherub.

questions.question: What was the measurements of the molten sea?
questions.correct_answer: Ten cubits by ten cubits and five cubits high
options: Ten cubits by ten cubits and five cubits high
questions.explanation: The molten sea was ten cubits in diameter and five cubits high.

questions.question: What was under the molten sea?
questions.explanation: The molten sea stood upon twelve oxen.

questions.question: How many lavers did Solomon make?
questions.explanation: Solomon made ten lavers in the temple court.

questions.question: What did Solomon place in the temple court besides the great court?
questions.correct_answer: Doors for the court overlaid with brass
options: Doors for the court overlaid with brass
questions.explanation: Solomon made the doors of the court and overlaid them with brass.

questions.question: Where was the molten sea set?
questions.correct_answer: On the right side of the east end, over against the south
options: On the right side of the east end, over against the south
questions.explanation: The molten sea was placed at the right side of the east end, opposite the south.

questions.question: Who made the pots, shovels, and basins for the temple?
questions.explanation: Huram made the vessels required for the temple service.

questions.question: How many pomegranates did Solomon make on the two wreaths atop the pillars?
questions.explanation: There were four hundred pomegranates on the two wreaths of the pillars.

questions.question: How many oxen supported the molten sea?
questions.explanation: The sea stood on twelve oxen.

questions.question: What material were the candlesticks made of?
questions.explanation: The candlesticks were made of gold for the temple.

questions.question: Which parts of the house were made of pure gold, not silver?
questions.correct_answer: All the drinking vessels and all the vessels of the house of the forest of Lebanon
options: All the drinking vessels and all the vessels of the house of the forest of Lebanon
questions.explanation: All of Solomon's drinking vessels and forest house vessels were pure gold.

questions.question: Who assembled the elders and heads of tribes to bring up the ark?
questions.explanation: Solomon assembled the leaders of Israel to bring up the ark.

questions.question: Who brought up the ark and holy vessels in the procession?
questions.explanation: The priests and Levites carried the ark and holy vessels.

questions.question: How many oxen were sacrificed when the ark was brought into its place?
questions.correct_answer: A number too many to tell or number
options: A number too many to tell or number
questions.explanation: The sacrifices were so numerous they could not be numbered.

questions.question: Where was the ark of the covenant placed inside the temple?
questions.correct_answer: In the oracle of the house, into the most holy place
options: In the oracle of the house, into the most holy place
In the court of the priests
questions.explanation: The ark was placed in the most holy place, under the cherubim.

questions.question: What was in the ark when it was brought to the temple?
questions.correct_answer: The two tables of stone with the covenant
options: The two tables of stone with the covenant
questions.explanation: The ark contained the two tablets that Moses put there at Horeb.

questions.question: What filled the house of the LORD when the trumpeters and singers praised Him?
questions.explanation: The glory of the LORD filled the house like a cloud.

questions.question: What did Solomon say the LORD had said about dwelling in darkness?
questions.correct_answer: The LORD would dwell in thick darkness
options: The LORD would dwell in thick darkness
The LORD would dwell in the light
The LORD would dwell among the people
The LORD would dwell in the heavens
questions.explanation: Solomon acknowledged that God would dwell in thick darkness.

questions.question: Why did Solomon say he had built an house of habitation for God?
questions.correct_answer: To provide a place for God's dwelling forever
options: To provide a place for God's dwelling forever
questions.explanation: Solomon built a permanent dwelling place for the Lord.

questions.question: Which city did God choose among all tribes of Israel to put His name?
questions.explanation: God chose Jerusalem among all tribes for His name.

questions.question: Who did God choose to be ruler over his people Israel?
questions.explanation: God chose David to rule Israel, as Solomon said in his prayer.

questions.question: What question did Solomon ask regarding God dwelling on earth?
questions.correct_answer: If God could dwell with men on earth since heaven cannot contain him
options: If God could dwell with men on earth since heaven cannot contain him
If God would forgive sins of the people
If God would accept offerings of animals
If God would choose another temple
questions.explanation: Solomon asks whether God would really dwell on earth, noting heaven cannot contain Him.

questions.question: What did Solomon ask God to do when His people pray toward the temple?
questions.correct_answer: Hear from heaven and forgive their sin
options: Hear from heaven and forgive their sin
questions.explanation: Solomon prayed for God to hear from heaven and forgive Israel when they confess.

questions.question: What did Solomon request concerning an oath made against a neighbor?
questions.correct_answer: That God would judge and requite the wicked and justify the righteous
options: That God would judge and requite the wicked and justify the righteous
That God would destroy the oath
That God would grant wealth to the one who swore
That God would send a prophet
questions.explanation: Solomon asked God to judge according to justice when oaths were sworn at the altar.

questions.question: What did Solomon ask God to do if Israel was defeated because of sin?
questions.correct_answer: Hear their confession and return them to the land
options: Hear their confession and return them to the land
questions.explanation: Solomon asked God to forgive and restore Israel if they repent after defeat.

questions.question: Which calamities did Solomon mention if heaven was shut up?
questions.correct_answer: No rain because they have sinned against God
options: No rain because they have sinned against God
questions.explanation: Solomon mentioned drought as a consequence of sin and pleaded for forgiveness.

questions.question: Which specific disasters did Solomon mention when asking God to hear prayer?
questions.correct_answer: Dearth, pestilence, blasting, mildew, locusts, or caterpillars
options: Dearth, pestilence, blasting, mildew, locusts, or caterpillars
questions.explanation: Solomon listed a range of calamities and asked that God hear prayers.

questions.question: What did Solomon say God should do for the stranger who prays in the temple?
questions.correct_answer: Hear him from heaven and do according to his request
options: Hear him from heaven and do according to his request
Turn him away because he is not Israelite
Require him to become Jewish first
questions.explanation: Solomon asked that God also hear foreigners who pray toward the temple.

questions.question: What did Solomon ask God to do if His people go to war by the way He sends them?
questions.correct_answer: Hear their prayer and maintain their cause
options: Hear their prayer and maintain their cause
questions.explanation: Solomon prayed that God would hear Israel's war prayers when they obey His way.

questions.question: What did Solomon say would happen if Israel sinned and was carried captive?
questions.correct_answer: They could pray from captivity and be heard if they repented
options: They could pray from captivity and be heard if they repented
They would remain in captivity forever
They should flee to another land
questions.explanation: Solomon asked that God hear repentant captives when they pray toward their land and temple.

questions.question: What did Solomon ask God to keep open when prayers are made in the temple?
questions.explanation: Solomon asked God to keep His eyes and ears attentive to prayer.

questions.question: What did Solomon ask God not to turn away?
questions.explanation: Solomon asked God to remember the mercies of David and not turn away from His anointed.

questions.question: What came down from heaven after Solomon finished praying?
questions.explanation: Fire came down from heaven and consumed the offerings after Solomon prayed.

questions.question: Why could the priests not enter the house of the LORD after the fire came down?
questions.correct_answer: The glory of the LORD filled the house
options: The glory of the LORD filled the house
The people were not holy enough
questions.explanation: The priests could not enter because the glory of the Lord filled the house.

questions.question: How did the people respond when the glory of the LORD filled the house?
questions.correct_answer: They bowed with faces to the ground and worshipped
options: They bowed with faces to the ground and worshipped
questions.explanation: The people bowed and worshiped when they saw the glory.

questions.question: How many oxen did Solomon offer when dedicating the house?
questions.explanation: Solomon offered twenty-two thousand oxen during the dedication of the house.

questions.question: How many sheep did Solomon offer when dedicating the house?
questions.explanation: Solomon offered one hundred twenty thousand sheep.

questions.question: What instruments did the Levites use when praising the LORD?
questions.correct_answer: Instruments of music made by David
options: Instruments of music made by David
questions.explanation: The Levites praised God with musical instruments made by David.

questions.question: How long did Solomon keep the dedication feast?
questions.explanation: Solomon kept the dedication of the altar for seven days.

questions.question: How long was the solemn assembly on the eighth day?
questions.correct_answer: One day after the seven-day feast
options: One day after the seven-day feast
questions.explanation: On the eighth day they made a solemn assembly after seven days of dedication.

questions.question: What was the mood of the people when they were sent away after the dedication?
questions.explanation: The people left joyful and thankful.

questions.question: Who appeared to Solomon by night after the dedication?
questions.explanation: God appeared to Solomon by night and spoke to him after the dedication.

questions.question: What threats did God mention He might send if He shut up heaven?
questions.explanation: God warned of drought, locusts, and pestilence if Israel forsook Him.

questions.question: What must the people do so God will hear and heal their land?
questions.correct_answer: Humble themselves, pray, seek His face, and turn from wicked ways
options: Humble themselves, pray, seek His face, and turn from wicked ways
questions.explanation: God promised restoration if His people humbled themselves and repented.

questions.question: What would happen to the house if Israel turned away and served other gods?
questions.correct_answer: God would cast it out of His sight and make it a proverb and byword
options: God would cast it out of His sight and make it a proverb and byword
God would leave it intact forever
God would hide it from the nations
questions.explanation: God warned that the temple would become despised if His people forsook Him.

questions.question: How many years had passed since Solomon built the house of the LORD when the cities were restored?
questions.explanation: Solomon had built the temple and his own house by the end of twenty years.

questions.question: What city did Solomon build in the wilderness?
questions.explanation: Solomon built Tadmor in the wilderness.

questions.question: Which two cities of Judah did Solomon build as fenced cities?
questions.correct_answer: Beth-horon the upper and Beth-horon the nether
options: Beth-horon the upper and Beth-horon the nether
questions.explanation: He built the upper and nether Beth-horon as fortified cities.

questions.question: Which people remaining in the land did Solomon make pay tribute?
questions.correct_answer: Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites
options: Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites
questions.explanation: Solomon made the remaining inhabitants pay tribute rather than enslave Israelites.

questions.question: How many chief officers did Solomon have?
questions.explanation: Solomon had two hundred and fifty chief officers.

questions.question: How many talents of gold did Solomon receive from Ophir?
questions.explanation: Solomon received four hundred and fifty talents of gold from Ophir.

questions.question: Who came to Solomon to test him with hard questions?
questions.explanation: The queen of Sheba visited Solomon with hard questions.

questions.question: What did the queen of Sheba say about Solomon after seeing his wisdom?
questions.correct_answer: There was no more spirit in her
options: There was no more spirit in her
She refused to speak to him
questions.explanation: Sheba was overwhelmed by Solomon's wisdom and riches.

questions.question: What did the queen of Sheba declare about Solomon's wisdom?
questions.correct_answer: The one half of the greatness of thy wisdom was not told me
options: The one half of the greatness of thy wisdom was not told me
He was no wiser than others
He was a mighty king only in war
questions.explanation: Sheba acknowledged that half of Solomon's greatness had not been told to her.

questions.question: Whom did the queen of Sheba bless for loving Solomon and setting him on his throne?
questions.explanation: Sheba blessed Solomon's God for making him king with justice.

questions.question: What gifts did the queen of Sheba bring to Solomon?
questions.explanation: She brought gold, spices, and precious stones to Solomon.

questions.question: What did Solomon make from the algum trees brought from Ophir?
questions.correct_answer: Terraces to the house of the LORD and to the king's palace, and harps and psalteries for singers
options: Terraces to the house of the LORD and to the king's palace, and harps and psalteries for singers
questions.explanation: Solomon used the precious wood to make terraces and musical instruments.

questions.question: How much gold came to Solomon in one year?
questions.explanation: Solomon received six hundred sixty-six talents of gold in one year.

questions.question: How much gold went to make one of Solomon's shields?
questions.explanation: Each gold target cost six hundred shekels.

questions.question: How many shields of beaten gold did Solomon make?
questions.explanation: Solomon made three hundred shields of beaten gold.

questions.question: What was Solomon's throne made of?
questions.explanation: Solomon's throne was ivory overlaid with pure gold.

questions.question: How many lions stood by Solomon's throne on the six steps?
questions.explanation: Twelve lions stood by the throne on the six steps.

questions.question: What material were all Solomon's drinking vessels made of?
questions.explanation: All the drinking vessels were made of gold.

questions.question: How often did the ships of Tarshish come bringing goods?
questions.explanation: The ships of Tarshish brought goods every three years.

questions.question: From which river to the border of Egypt did Solomon reign?
questions.explanation: Solomon reigned from the river to the border of Egypt.

questions.question: How long did Solomon reign in Jerusalem?
questions.explanation: Solomon reigned forty years in Jerusalem.

questions.question: Who reigned after Solomon died?
questions.explanation: Solomon's son Rehoboam reigned after him.

questions.question: Where did Rehoboam go to be made king?
questions.explanation: Rehoboam went to Shechem where all Israel gathered to crown him king.

questions.question: From where did Jeroboam return when Israel called him?
questions.explanation: Jeroboam had fled to Egypt and returned when the people sought Rehoboam.

questions.question: What request did Israel make of Rehoboam regarding the yoke?
questions.correct_answer: Ease somewhat the grievous servitude of his father
options: Ease somewhat the grievous servitude of his father
questions.explanation: They asked Rehoboam to lighten Solomon's heavy yoke.

questions.question: Whose counsel did Rehoboam first seek about answering the people?
questions.correct_answer: The old men who stood before Solomon
options: The old men who stood before Solomon
The young men of his court
questions.explanation: Rehoboam first consulted the elder advisers who served Solomon.

questions.question: What counsel did the old men give Rehoboam?
questions.correct_answer: If thou be kind to this people and speak good words, they will serve thee forever
options: If thou be kind to this people and speak good words, they will serve thee forever
Show them strength and make them fear
Tax them heavier than thy father did
Send them back to their tents
questions.explanation: The elders told Rehoboam to be kind and gentle with the people.

questions.question: Whom did Rehoboam seek counsel from after rejecting the old men?
questions.correct_answer: The young men who were brought up with him
options: The young men who were brought up with him
questions.explanation: Rehoboam consulted the young men raised with him instead of the older advisors.

questions.question: What response did Rehoboam give to Israel after taking the young men's advice?
questions.correct_answer: My father chastised you with whips, but I will chastise you with scorpions
options: My father chastised you with whips, but I will chastise you with scorpions
My father loved you, and so do I
Take your yoke and be content
questions.explanation: Rehoboam declared his punishment would be harsher than his father's.

questions.question: What did Israel say to Rehoboam when he would not listen?
questions.correct_answer: What portion have we in David? every man to your tents
options: What portion have we in David? every man to your tents
We will fight to keep the kingdom
We will obey you despite your words
We will seek another king from Judah
questions.explanation: The northern tribes rejected Rehoboam and returned to their tents.

questions.question: Which tribes remained under Rehoboam's rule?
questions.explanation: Only Judah and Benjamin stayed loyal to Rehoboam.

questions.question: Who was stoned to death after Rehoboam sent him to the people?
questions.explanation: Hadoram, who supervised tribute, was stoned by Israel.

questions.question: How many chosen warriors did Rehoboam gather from Judah and Benjamin?
questions.explanation: Rehoboam gathered one hundred eighty thousand warriors to fight Israel.

questions.question: Who told Rehoboam not to fight against Israel because the matter was from the Lord?
questions.explanation: Shemaiah told Rehoboam that the split of the kingdom was God's doing.

questions.question: What did Rehoboam do after hearing Shemaiah's word?
questions.correct_answer: Returned home and obeyed the Lord's command
options: Returned home and obeyed the Lord's command
questions.explanation: He obeyed and returned from fighting his brethren.

questions.question: Which two kingdoms did Rehoboam build cities for defense in?
questions.explanation: He built defensive cities in Judah and Benjamin.

questions.question: Which city is among those Rehoboam built for defense?
questions.explanation: Bethlehem was one of the defended cities Rehoboam built.

questions.question: Why did the priests and Levites come to Rehoboam from all Israel?
questions.correct_answer: Jeroboam had cast them off from executing the priest's office
options: Jeroboam had cast them off from executing the priest's office
They wanted to join Rehoboam's court
questions.explanation: Jeroboam rejected the Levites and priests, so they went to Judah.

questions.question: For how many years did the kingdom of Judah remain strong under Rehoboam?
questions.explanation: Judah was strong for three years because they walked in the ways of David and Solomon.

questions.question: Who was Rehoboam's first wife?
questions.explanation: Mahalath is named as Rehoboam's wife.

questions.question: How many wives and concubines did Rehoboam have?
questions.explanation: Rehoboam had eighteen wives and sixty concubines.

questions.question: Who did Rehoboam make chief among his brethren?
questions.explanation: Rehoboam made Abijah, son of Maachah, chief among his brethren.

questions.question: What did Rehoboam do with his children throughout Judah and Benjamin?
questions.correct_answer: Dispersed them to every fenced city and gave them victual in abundance
options: Dispersed them to every fenced city and gave them victual in abundance
Sent them to serve in the temple
questions.explanation: He placed his children in fortified cities and supplied them well.

questions.question: What did Rehoboam do after he established the kingdom and strengthened himself?
questions.correct_answer: Forsook the law of the LORD
options: Forsook the law of the LORD
questions.explanation: Rehoboam turned away from God's law after strengthening his kingdom.

questions.question: Why did Shishak king of Egypt come up against Jerusalem?
questions.correct_answer: Because they had transgressed against the LORD
options: Because they had transgressed against the LORD
questions.explanation: Shishak invaded because Judah had turned away from God.

questions.question: How many chariots did Shishak bring to Jerusalem?
questions.explanation: Shishak brought twelve hundred chariots.

questions.question: How many horsemen accompanied Shishak?
questions.explanation: He brought sixty thousand horsemen among his forces.

questions.question: Which peoples came with Shishak from Egypt?
questions.explanation: Shishak's army included Lubims, Sukkiims, and Ethiopians.

questions.question: What did Shishak take when he came to Jerusalem?
questions.correct_answer: The treasures of the house of the LORD and the king's house
options: The treasures of the house of the LORD and the king's house
questions.explanation: Shishak took all the treasures from both the temple and the royal palace.

questions.question: What did Rehoboam make instead of the gold shields taken by Shishak?
questions.explanation: Rehoboam made brass shields to replace those taken by Shishak.

questions.question: Who fetched the shields when the king entered the house of the LORD?
questions.explanation: The guard brought the shields when Rehoboam entered the temple.

questions.question: What happened after Rehoboam humbled himself when Shishak invaded?
questions.correct_answer: The wrath of the LORD turned from him and he was not destroyed altogether
options: The wrath of the LORD turned from him and he was not destroyed altogether
questions.explanation: Because he humbled himself, God spared him from total destruction.

questions.question: How old was Rehoboam when he began to reign?
questions.explanation: Rehoboam was forty-one when he began to reign.

questions.question: How many years did Rehoboam reign in Jerusalem?
questions.explanation: Rehoboam reigned seventeen years in Jerusalem.

questions.question: Why did Rehoboam do evil according to the chronicle?
questions.correct_answer: Because he prepared not his heart to seek the LORD
options: Because he prepared not his heart to seek the LORD
Because he did not build enough cities
questions.explanation: His evil came from failing to set his heart to seek God.

questions.question: Where are the acts of Rehoboam written according to the book?
questions.correct_answer: The book of Shemaiah the prophet and of Iddo the seer
options: The book of Shemaiah the prophet and of Iddo the seer
The book of Samuel the seer
The book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah
The book of the kings of Israel
questions.explanation: The acts are written in the works of Shemaiah and Iddo.

questions.question: Who reigned after Rehoboam died?
questions.explanation: Abijah succeeded his father Rehoboam.

questions.question: In which year of Jeroboam did Abijah begin to reign over Judah?
questions.explanation: Abijah began to reign in Jeroboam's eighteenth year.

questions.question: How many years did Abijah reign in Jerusalem?
questions.explanation: Abijah reigned three years in Jerusalem.

questions.question: How many chosen men did Abijah have in his army?
questions.explanation: Abijah's army included four hundred thousand chosen men.

questions.question: How many chosen men did Jeroboam have in his army?
questions.explanation: Jeroboam's army numbered eight hundred thousand chosen men.

questions.question: What kind of covenant did God make with David?
questions.explanation: Abijah referred to the covenant of salt God made with David.

questions.question: What did Jeroboam make for Israel that Abijah condemned as gods?
questions.explanation: Abijah criticized Jeroboam for making golden calves and idols.

questions.question: What did the Levites and priests in Judah keep before the Lord?
questions.correct_answer: Burnt sacrifices morning and evening and the shewbread ordered on the table
options: Burnt sacrifices morning and evening and the shewbread ordered on the table
questions.explanation: They maintained the temple worship as commanded by God.

questions.question: Who did Abijah say was with Judah for their captain?
questions.explanation: Abijah declared that God was with Judah for their captain.

questions.question: What happened to the Israelites when they faced Judah in battle?
questions.correct_answer: They were caught between the army and an ambush behind them
options: They were caught between the army and an ambush behind them
They broke through and fled Judah
questions.explanation: Israel was ambushed and then trapped by Judah in battle.

questions.question: What cry did Judah raise when the priests sounded with trumpets?
questions.explanation: The men of Judah gave a shout and God struck Israel before them.

questions.question: How many chosen men of Israel were slain by Judah?
questions.explanation: Judah slew five hundred thousand chosen men of Israel.

questions.question: Which cities did Abijah take from Jeroboam after the battle?
questions.explanation: Abijah captured Beth-el and other cities from Jeroboam.

questions.question: How did Jeroboam's strength fare after this battle?
questions.correct_answer: He did not recover strength again and died
options: He did not recover strength again and died
questions.explanation: Jeroboam weakened after the defeat and later died.

questions.question: How many wives did Abijah have?
questions.explanation: Abijah had fourteen wives.

questions.question: How many sons did Abijah father?
questions.explanation: Abijah fathered twenty-two sons.

questions.question: Where are the acts and sayings of Abijah recorded?
questions.correct_answer: In the story of the prophet Iddo
options: In the story of the prophet Iddo
In the book of the kings of Israel
In the book of Shemaiah the prophet
questions.explanation: The chronicler notes that more about Abijah is written in Iddo's story.
